pi_items.source: Task scripts (setup_task.sh line 21, export_result.sh line 14) and task.json metadata
OpenICE documentation confirms simulated multiparameter monitors are available in Supervisor demo applications (search results from openice.info/docs/3_apps.html)
OpenICE documentation and search results explicitly confirm 'Simulated Pulse Oximeter' device is available (https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp and openice.info documentation)
OpenICE documentation confirms simulated pump device is available in Supervisor (search results from openice.info/docs/3_apps.html and mdpnp GitHub releases)

pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE is open-source Java-based medical device interoperability platform from MD PnP (Medical Device Plug-and-Play) program at Massachusetts General Hospital/Harvard Medical School

privileged_info_summary: Ward monitoring configuration task uses OpenICE (Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment), a real open-source medical device interoperability platform from MD PnP at Harvard/MGH. All three required device types (Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, Infusion Pump) are confirmed to be available as simulated devices in OpenICE Supervisor. The CSV output path is correctly specified as /home/ga/Desktop/ward_config.csv, and OpenICE logs to /home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log. The task involves synthetic data - creating simulated devices for testing interoperability, not analyzing real patient data.
